narration: Here's why you know you should save but still don't. Scientists call it present bias — your brain literally can't feel your future self's pain. And it costs you three hundred thousand dollars.
visual: Human brain SVG with two sides labeled 'TODAY' (bright, lit up) and 'FUTURE' (dark, faded). 'PRESENT BIAS' text springs in large over the brain. Tagline 'YOUR BRAIN'S MOST EXPENSIVE BUG' fades in below.

narration: Fifty-six percent of Americans have under a thousand dollars saved. Not because they're lazy. Researchers found your brain treats your future self like a total stranger — you literally don't feel their suffering.
visual: Row of 10 person silhouettes. Six of them dim out, leaving only 4 fully lit. Counter shows '56% HAVE UNDER $1,000 SAVED'. Piggy bank SVG slides in nearly empty with just coins rattling at the bottom.

narration: Here's the proof. Researchers asked people to choose between a hundred dollars today or more money in a year. To wait, your brain demanded two hundred and fifty-seven dollars. That's a hundred and fifty-seven percent return — no investment pays that.
visual: Two option boxes appear side by side: '$100 TODAY' with a glowing border vs '$257 IN 1 YEAR' with a dull border. A scale/balance SVG tips heavily toward the left. '157% RETURN REQUIRED' label pulses in blue.

narration: This is called hyperbolic discounting. The further away something is, the more your brain ignores it. Retirement thirty years out? Your brain treats it like it basically doesn't exist.
visual: Horizontal timeline from 'TODAY' to '30 YEARS'. A person silhouette stands at TODAY. The future portion gets progressively more faded and transparent. 'RETIREMENT' label at the far end is nearly invisible. 'YOUR BRAIN IGNORES THIS' arrow points to it.

narration: The math is brutal. Delay investing by just five years in your twenties and you forfeit around three hundred and twelve thousand dollars in compound growth. That's more than most people's homes are worth.
visual: Two investment growth curves animate upward side by side: 'START AT 25' curve towering to $312K higher than 'START AT 30' curve. The gap between them fills in blue. '$312,000 FORFEITED' label appears in the gap.

narration: The fix isn't willpower — it's automation. Set up an automatic transfer so future-you gets paid before present-you can spend it. That one decision is worth more than any budget you'll ever write.
visual: Phone SVG showing an automated transfer setup screen. Green arrows flow automatically from a wallet icon to a piggy bank icon labeled 'FUTURE YOU'. Calendar shows 'AUTO' badges on recurring dates. '$312,000 PROTECTED' badge springs in at the bottom.
